name: Patey Rock
region: North America, Canada, British Columbia, Vancouver Island
ocean: Pacific Ocean, Haro Strait, Saanich Inlet
description: This site in Saanich Inlet offers dramatic walls and pinnacles covered in colorful soft corals and sponges. Strong currents bring nutrient-rich waters, attracting wolf eels, giant Pacific octopus, and schools of rockfish. The rocky terrain creates exciting swim-throughs and overhangs. Visibility varies but rewards patient divers with abundant marine life. Cold water diving requires proper thermal protection. Best suited for intermediate to advanced divers due to currents.

name: Saxe Point
region: North America, Canada, British Columbia, Vancouver Island, Victoria
ocean: Pacific Ocean, Strait Of Juan De Fuca
description: Saxe Point offers gentle shore diving with rocky reefs and sandy patches. Visibility ranges 5-15 meters, best at slack tide. Spot giant Pacific octopus, wolf eels, and colorful nudibranchs among the kelp. Currents can be strong, making it ideal for drift diving. Depths reach 18 meters, suitable for all levels. The site is easily accessible from Victoria.

ocean: Pacific Ocean, Haro Strait, Saanich Inlet
description: A submerged pinnacle rises from the depths to within 30 feet of the surface, surrounded by vibrant marine life. Strong currents attract schools of rockfish and lingcod, while colorful anemones and sponges cover the rocky slopes. Divers can explore dramatic drop-offs and overhangs teeming with invertebrates. Cold but rewarding, this site suits intermediate divers comfortable with mild currents.

description: A sunken former Chinese smuggling vessel lies at 30 meters in Haro Strait. The wreck is encrusted with plumose anemones and surrounded by lingcod, rockfish, and wolf eels. Strong currents bring excellent visibility but require slack tide dives. The intact structure offers swim-throughs and penetration opportunities for advanced divers. Nearby reefs host colorful invertebrates and schools of perch. Cold water demands proper thermal protection.

description: A vibrant shore dive near Victoria with easy access and year-round diving. The breakwater creates a sheltered environment teeming with marine life—giant Pacific octopus, wolf eels, and colorful nudibranchs. Rocky reefs and artificial structures host dense kelp forests. Strong currents can occur, but visibility often reaches 30 feet. Ideal for macro photography and night dives.

description: Strong currents sweep through this rocky outcrop, creating a nutrient-rich haven for marine life. Visibility often exceeds 50 feet, but cold water and surge demand experience. Tidal exchanges create dynamic conditions perfect for advanced divers seeking adrenaline and abundant biodiversity.

description: Setchel Point offers a dramatic wall dive in Saanich Inlet, dropping to 100 feet with vibrant plumose anemones and cloud sponges. Strong currents bring nutrient-rich waters, attracting wolf eels, octopus, and schools of rockfish. The steep slope transitions to a sandy bottom at depth. Best dived during slack tide for visibility up to 40 feet. Cold water year-round requires proper thermal protection. Nearby kelp forests shelter juvenile fish and invertebrates. Accessible by boat from local marinas.

name: White Lady
region: North America, Canada, British Columbia, Vancouver Island
ocean: Pacific Ocean, Haro Strait, Saanich Inlet
description: A stunning wall dive in Saanich Inlet, known for its vibrant white plumose anemones covering the rock face. The steep drop-off starts shallow, making it accessible for all levels. Expect excellent visibility and cold water teeming with rockfish, lingcod, and occasional wolf eels. The current can be strong, so check conditions. Unique for its dense anemone garden resembling a flowing white curtain. Best dived at slack tide.
